questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q: For the phenomenon of adsorption, the correct statement/s among the following is /are
options: According to Freundlich model, mass of $\mathrm{N}_2$ gas adsorbed per g of silica surface will increase with temperature of adsorption

If S is the surface area of an adsorbent, and ' A ', ' m ' and ' M ' are the cross-sectional area, mass adsorbed and molar mass of the adsorbate respectively, then $S=\left(\frac{m}{M}\right) A N_A$ ( $\mathrm{N}_{\mathrm{A}}$-Avogadro's number)
The number of gas molecules physisorbed on unit mass of an adsorbent will be the same for two different gases at the same temperature.
Adsorption of $\mathrm{H}_2$ on Ni surface with $\mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{a}}=96 \mathrm{~kJ} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$ can be termed as chemisorption

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q: Choose the correct statement(s) regarding zeolites:
options: Silicon atoms are replaced by aluminium atoms in the zeolites.
The pores and cavities of the zeolites as well as size and shape of reactant decides the reactions taking place in the zeolites.
The cracking of hydrocarbons and isomerisation reactions are catalyzed by zeolites in the petrochemical industries.
Zeolites act as molecular sieves and can separate the molecules of different sizes.

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q: The correct statement/s among the following is/are:
options: The charge on the diffused layer of AgI colloidal solution by the addition of few drops of dilute aqueous solution of KI to an aqueous solution of $\mathrm{AgNO}_3$ is negative.
The charge on the diffused layer of AgI colloidal solution by the addition of few drops of dilute aqueous solution of $\mathrm{AgNO}_3$ to an aqueous solution of KI is positive.
When the ionic strength of a colloidal solution is increased, thickness of the double layer is increased, and the colloid gets precipitated.
When the ionic strength of a colloidal solution is increased, thickness of the double layer is decreased, and the colloid gets precipitated.
